chunks.page_summary: This page introduces WirelessHART communication for Rheonics inline viscometers and density sensors, outlining the products involved, the article's purpose, and a detailed table of contents for setup and configuration, along with a definition and benefits of WirelessHART.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Gettng Started with WirelessHART Communication

Modified on: Fri, 21 Nov, 2025 at 9:10 PM

What products are involved?
Rheonics SRV viscomete (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), SRD density-viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), DVP gas density-viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/), and DVM HPHT density-viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/).

What is the purpose of this article?
This article explains the option of using WirelessHART with Rheonics inline viscometers, the necessary hardware, how it connects, and how it is configured. WirelessHART is a wireless extension of the HART protocol, fully compatible with existing HART devices.

Table of Contents
1. What is WirelessHART?
2. Required Hardware for Rheonics WirelessHART Setup
    2.1 HART Device: Rheonics SME
    2.2 WirelessHART Adapter
    2.3 WirelessHART Gateway
3. How to Configure WirelessHART Devices
    3.1 Wireless Gateway
        3.1.1 Installation and Wiring
        3.1.2 Network Settings
    3.2 Wireless Adapter Setup and Configuration
        3.2.1 WirelessHART Adapter Wiring
        3.2.2 Network Settings
        3.2.3 Wired Device Settings
        3.2.4 Broadcast Settings
    3.3 SME Configuration
    3.4 Functionality Check
4. Gateway Data Integration
5. References

1. What is WirelessHART?
WirelessHART (https://www.fieldcommgroup.org/technologies/wirelesshart) is a radio communication protocol that extends the widely adopted HART (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000418933) standard by adding wireless capability while keeping full compatibility with existing HART devices. The technology uses a self-organizing mesh network, where every device can act both as a measurement point and as a repeater.

Key benefits include:
* Reduced cabling and installation costs.
* Faster deployment of new measurement points.
* Access to remote or difficult-to-wire areas
* Full visibility of HART diagnostics and multivariable data
key_facts: WirelessHART is a radio communication protocol that extends the widely adopted HART standard.
WirelessHART adds wireless capability while keeping full compatibility with existing HART devices.
The technology uses a self-organizing mesh network.
Every WirelessHART device can act both as a measurement point and as a repeater.
Key benefits of WirelessHART include reduced cabling and installation costs.
Key benefits of WirelessHART include faster deployment of new measurement points.
Key benefits of WirelessHART include access to remote or difficult-to-wire areas.
Key benefits of WirelessHART include full visibility of HART diagnostics and multivariable data.

chunks.page_summary: This page outlines the required hardware components and setup for integrating Rheonics inline viscometers and density meters into a WirelessHART network, detailing the role of Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) as the HART device.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 1. WirelessHART- Image Sourced from FieldComm Group Website
2. Required Hardware for Rheonics WirelessHART Setup
To enable Rheonics inline viscometers (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) and density meters (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/) to communicate wirelessly using WirelessHART, the following hardware components are required:
• HART Device
• WirelessHART Adapter
• WirelessHART Gateway
Figure 2. WirelessHART Setup
Customers can use their preferred vendor's WirelessHART adapters and gateways. Rheonics provides the HART device (SME), while the wireless infrastructure (adapters and gateways) can be sourced from other vendors.
2.1 HART Device: Rheonics SME
Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) (SME) are robust, high-speed electronics paired with Rheonics sensors. They convert probe signals into viscosity, density, and temperature, and transmit data via multiple protocols, including HART (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412295).
key_facts: Rheonics inline viscometers and density meters can communicate wirelessly using WirelessHART.
The required hardware components for a WirelessHART setup include a HART Device, WirelessHART Adapter, and WirelessHART Gateway.
Customers have the flexibility to use their preferred vendor's WirelessHART adapters and gateways.
Rheonics provides the HART device, which is their Smart Module Electronics (SME).
Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) are robust, high-speed electronics designed to be paired with Rheonics sensors.
SME convert probe signals into measurements of viscosity, density, and temperature.
SME transmit data via multiple protocols, including HART, Modbus TCP, EtherNet/IP, and OPC UA.
Figure 1, illustrating the WirelessHART network, is sourced from the FieldComm Group Website.
diagram_labels: Industrial Protocol: Modbus TCP, EtherNet/IP, OPC UA.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the components and functionality of Rheonics' WirelessHART integration, detailing the adapter and gateway for their inline viscometers and density sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 3. Rheonics SME TRD with WirelessHART Adapter

Rheonics offers a preconfigured WirelessHART Adapter ready to use out of the box.

2.2 WirelessHART Adapter
The HART Adapter connects to the SME and transmits the HART signal wirelessly within a WirelessHART network. In this example, the adapter used is Emerson Wireless 775 THUM™ Adapter.

Figure 4. WirelessHART Adapter

2.3 WirelessHART Gateway
The Gateway is the central hub of a WirelessHART network. It collects data from WirelessHART devices and makes it available to control systems. The gateway translates WirelessHART into standard industrial protocols such as EtherNet/IP (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412288) or Modbus TCP (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297635); allowing PLCs, DCS, and SCADA systems to read sensor data without native HART support.

Dens: 1.120 g/cc
Temp: 79.851 °F
811-0103 V04.00/1 19101442
[Buttons/icons on Rheonics SME TRD]: Power, Setup, Monitor, HART, Bluetooth
[Text on Rheonics SME TRD casing]: rheonics, KEEP COVER TIGHT WHEN CIRCUITS ARE ALIVE
[Text on Emerson WirelessHART Adapter in Figure 4]: EMERSON
key_facts: Rheonics provides a preconfigured WirelessHART Adapter that is ready for immediate use.
The HART Adapter connects to the SME (sensor) and wirelessly transmits the HART signal within a WirelessHART network.
The Emerson Wireless 775 THUM™ Adapter is cited as an example of a compatible HART adapter.
The WirelessHART Gateway functions as the central hub of a WirelessHART network.
The gateway collects data from WirelessHART devices and makes it available to control systems.
The gateway translates WirelessHART data into standard industrial protocols such as EtherNet/IP or Modbus TCP.
This translation allows PLCs, DCS, and SCADA systems to read sensor data even if they lack native HART support.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a guide on how to configure WirelessHART devices, outlining the installation, wiring, and network settings required to integrate a Rheonics HART-enabled viscometer into a WirelessHART network.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 5. WirelessHART Gateway

3. How to Configure WirelessHART Devices

To integrate a HART-enabled Rheonics viscometer into a WirelessHART network, both the gateway and the wireless adapter must be configured with the same Network Settings. While the exact steps may differ slightly depending on the vendor, the overall procedure follows the same principles across all WirelessHART-compliant devices.

3.1 Wireless Gateway

3.1.1 Installation and Wiring

Follow the manufacturer's instructions to wire and install the WirelessHART gateway. Once it is powered on and accessible through its web interface, you can proceed with the configuration.

Figure 6. WirelessHART Gateway and Antenna

3.1.2 Network Settings

The main configuration step for a WirelessHART Gateway is setting up the Network ID and the Join Key.

*   Network ID: Identifies the WirelessHART network (e.g., 6124).
*   Join Key: A shared security key required by all devices that will join the network.

Both parameters must also be configured on the THUM adapter (or any other WirelessHART device) so it can successfully connect to the gateway.
key_facts: HART-enabled Rheonics viscometers can be integrated into a WirelessHART network.
Both the gateway and the wireless adapter require configuration with identical Network Settings.
The overall configuration procedure for WirelessHART-compliant devices follows consistent principles, irrespective of minor vendor-specific step variations.
The WirelessHART gateway must be properly wired, installed, powered on, and its web interface accessible prior to configuration.
The primary configuration tasks for a WirelessHART Gateway involve setting the Network ID and the Join Key.
The Network ID uniquely identifies the WirelessHART network.
The Join Key acts as a shared security key, mandatory for all devices attempting to join the network.
The Network ID and Join Key must also be configured on the THUM adapter (or any other WirelessHART device) to ensure successful connection to the gateway.
warnings_notes: While the exact steps may differ slightly depending on the vendor, the overall procedure follows the same principles across all WirelessHART-compliant devices.
Once the WirelessHART gateway is powered on and accessible through its web interface, you can proceed with the configuration.

Figure 7. Network Settings of 1410S Gateway

Figure 7 shows the web browser interface of an Emerson Wireless Gateway 1410S. The user can log in with the default IP address and credentials to configure the network settings under the Network tab.

Because WirelessHART is a standardized protocol, the customer is not restricted to a specific vendor’s gateway; they may use their own compatible [gateways].

3.2 Wireless Adapter Setup and Configuration
A WirelessHART adapter can be configured through different ways, such as:
* Using a Field Communicator (e.g., Emerson 475/AMS Trex) to enter the Network ID and Join Key.
* Using a HART modem connected to a PC, with configuration software such as PACTware and the appropriate DTM library.

The following configurations were performed using PACTware.

3.2.1 WirelessHART Adapter Wiring
The WirelessHART Adapter can be mounted directly on Rheonics TRD. The Smart Module Electronics (SME) provides HART communication, while the HART Adapter transmits this data wirelessly over the WirelessHART network.

Rheonics SME is a 4-Wire Active device. Figure 8 shows a connection diagram where there is no 4-20 mA loop.

Figure 8. Emerson THUM Adapter Wiring.
Source: Emerson Wireless 775THUM Adapter Reference Manual, 00809-0100-4075, Rev CF, June 2023.

Additional configuration and mounting options for the Wireless Adapter are available. Please contact Rheonics Support for more information.

3.2.2 Network Settings
There are two critical parameters are on Network Settings:
* Network ID: identifies the WirelessHART network.
* Join Key: security key shared by all devices in the same network.

Both values must match the gateway settings for the Wireless Adapter to successfully join the network.
key_facts: The Emerson Wireless Gateway 1410S web browser interface allows users to configure network settings, accessible via a default IP address and credentials under the Network tab.
WirelessHART is a standardized protocol, meaning customers are not limited to a specific vendor's gateway and can use compatible alternatives.

WirelessHART adapters can be configured using either a Field Communicator (e.g., Emerson 475/AMS Trex) to enter Network ID and Join Key, or a HART modem connected to a PC with PACTware and DTM library.
The configurations mentioned were performed using PACTware.
The WirelessHART Adapter can be directly mounted on Rheonics TRD.
The Smart Module Electronics (SME) provides HART communication and transmits data wirelessly over the WirelessHART network.
Rheonics SME is a 4-Wire Active device.
The connection diagram for the Rheonics SME (Figure 8) does not include a 4-20 mA loop.
Critical network settings include Network ID, which identifies the WirelessHART network, and Join Key, a security key shared by all devices.
For a Wireless Adapter to successfully join the network, its Network ID and Join Key must match the gateway settings.
warnings_notes: Additional configuration and mounting options for the Wireless Adapter are available. Please contact Rheonics Support for more information.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the configuration of HART polling and broadcast settings for a THUM adapter using PACTware software to provide process variables to a WirelessHART network.
chunks.extracted_text:
Polling commands: configure the adapter to request at least Command 1 (Primary Variable) and/or Command 3 (Dynamic Variables). Command 3 is recommended because it retrieves PV, SV, TV, and QV in one response.

Figure 12. PACTware - Wired Device Settings: Configure HART Polling

Update rates: How frequently the adapter should issue the polling commands

Once these settings are applied, the adapter will continuously poll the SME via HART and provide the process variables to the WirelessHART network.

Rheonics HART Field Device specifications are available in the: Rheonics HART Manual (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Rheonics-HART-Man

3.2.4 Broadcast Settings
Once the THUM adapter has joined the network and is able to poll the HART device, you can configure the broadcast messages and update rates as follows:
- Message 1: All variables and device status.
- Message 2: Standard HART variables (PV, SV, TV, QV).
- Message 3: Device status only.

The update rate determines how often data packets are sent to the gateway. This can be adjusted depending on the process and monitoring requirements.

Figure 13. PACTware - Broadcast Settings
key_facts: Polling commands can be configured to request Command 1 (Primary Variable) and/or Command 3 (Dynamic Variables) from the HART device.
Command 3 is recommended for polling as it retrieves PV, SV, TV, and QV in a single response.
Once configured, the adapter continuously polls the SME via HART to provide process variables to the WirelessHART network.
Rheonics HART Field Device specifications are available in the Rheonics HART Manual.

The THUM adapter's broadcast messages can be configured to include different sets of data: all variables and device status (Message 1), standard HART variables (PV, SV, TV, QV) (Message 2), or device status only (Message 3).
Broadcast update rates dictate the frequency of data packet transmission to the gateway and are adjustable based on process and monitoring needs.
diagram_labels: Configure HART Polling-THUM V01 Wireless DTM 264F01h-Method
The THUM may transmit two types of information from the wired device.
Below is the current information that the THUM will transmit from the primary device.
Would you like to change this information?

chunks.page_summary: This page details the configuration and data integration process for Rheonics SME sensors with a WirelessHART Gateway, including functionality checks and steps to expose sensor data via common industrial protocols.
chunks.extracted_text:
3.3 SME Configuration
No additional configuration is needed to establish communication between the WirelessHART Adapter and the Rheonics SME.
If you are having issues with HART, take a look at HART Troubleshooting: Rheonics Support (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/)

3.4 Functionality Check
After the gateway, and adapter have been configured, the Rheonics sensor should appear on the gateway.
1. Access the **Gateway Web Interface**.
2. On the **Home** screen, the sensor should be listed as a newly added device (see Figure 14). Once the WirelessHART Adapter has been configured with the correct **Network ID** and **Join Key** and given sufficient time to join the network, the Rheonics SME will appear automatically when powered on.

Figure 14. Gateway Web Interface: New Device

4. Gateway Data Integration
With the sensor now visible on the gateway, the final step is to configure the gateway to expose this data over the plant's preferred communication protocol (such as Modbus TCP (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297635), EtherNet/IP (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412288) or OPC UA ).
By mapping the HART variables (PV, SV, TV, QV) to the chosen protocol, the sensor measurements become directly available to higher-level systems.

Figure 15. Gateway Web Interface: Data from Rheonics SME

5. References
*   Rheonics, "SRV Inline Viscometer." [Online]. Available: Rheonics SRV » inline online Viscometer for fluid viscosity monitoring (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].
*   Rheonics, "SRD Inline Density Meter." [Online]. Available: Rheonics SRD » Density Meter inline online specific gravity concentration (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].

*   FieldComm Group, "WirelessHART – Expanding the Possibilities" (Rev. 3.3 brochure). [Online]. Available: WirelessHART_Expanding_Possibilities_bro_Rev3.3.pdf (https://www.fieldcommgroup.org/sites/default/files/imce_files/technology/documents/WirelessHart_Expanding_Possibilities_bro_Rev3.3.pdf). [Accessed: Sep. 9, 2025].
key_facts: No additional configuration is needed to establish communication between the WirelessHART Adapter and the Rheonics SME.
After the gateway and adapter have been configured, the Rheonics sensor should appear on the gateway's Home screen as a newly added device.
The WirelessHART Adapter must be configured with the correct Network ID and Join Key.
Once the WirelessHART Adapter is configured and powered on, the Rheonics SME will appear automatically.
The gateway can be configured to expose sensor data, including HART variables (PV, SV, TV, QV), over plant communication protocols such as Modbus TCP, EtherNet/IP, or OPC UA.
Mapping HART variables to a chosen protocol makes sensor measurements directly available to higher-level systems.
